steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes a specific gap: filtration can't capture very small microplastics. The test results show the sound wave system removes particles down to 10 microns — the size range filtration misses. The correct inference connects those two facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'sound waves are more cost-effective than filtration': the passage never compares costs. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the sound wave system may help solve a problem associated with filtration of microplastics': the system removes what filtration can't — this directly bridges the passage's problem and the test result. ✓
  • Choice C — 'filtration will eventually be replaced by sound wave technology': too strong — the passage describes a test, not a replacement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'microplastics smaller than 10 microns pose no risk': contradicts the passage's concern about small microplastics. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
